Policy_Note: Highest predicted uninsured rate of any caste group; lowest baseline coverage; not targeted by caste-based schemes and often above BPL threshold
Highest regional uninsured rate; Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal — low BPL card penetration and awareness barriers dominate
High uninsured rate despite relatively high ST population (which has better coverage); state-level heterogeneity is high
Largest absolute gain but still >60% uninsured; BPL card is strongest predictor — enrollment drives access
High predicted uninsured despite wealth — suggests avoidance of public schemes; not a PM-JAY target group
Lower gains than rural; urban informally employed workers lack ESIS/CGHS access and are PM-JAY eligible but under-enrolled
Strong gains; still majority uninsured — gap between BPL eligibility and actual enrollment
Gains above national average; still majority uninsured; SHAP shows caste is a weaker predictor than wealth — wealth channels most of the SC disadvantage
Higher raw gains than urban but DiD negative — control states (Odisha BSKY) grew faster; PM-JAY rural outreach underperformed
Largest caste-group gains; still majority uninsured; large and heterogeneous group — sub-group targeting needed
Highest baseline coverage of all caste groups (state tribal schemes pre-PM-JAY); lowest predicted uninsured — relatively better off
